title: thymus teucrioides subsp. alpinus
intro: Thymus teucrioides subsp. alpinus, also known as alpine thyme or mountain thyme, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae, characterized by its small, white flowers and fragrant foliage. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which make it effective in treating respiratory issues, wounds, and digestive problems. The health benefits of Thymus teucrioides subsp. alpinus include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce stress and anxiety, and exhibit antioxidant properties. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of this plant are often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can also be used as a seasoning in cooking.
